items.title: Ransomware hackers claim breach at Foxconn, a major electronics manufacturer for Apple, Google, and Nvidia
items.content: A ransomware group has claimed responsibility for hacking the electronics manufacturing giant Foxconn and is attempting to extort the company.

items.title: Finally, texts between Android and iPhone users can be end-to-end encrypted
items.content: Google had urged Apple for years to support RCS texting to make communication between each company's devices more seamless.

items.title: Intel’s comeback story is even wilder than it seems
items.content: Intel's stock has risen a stunning 490% over the past year, a bet by Wall Street that may be running well ahead of the company's actual turnaround.

items.title: Apple to pay $250M to settle lawsuit over Siri’s delayed AI features
items.content: Apple has agreed to pay $250 million to settle a class action lawsuit for overpromising the arrival of Siri's AI features.

items.title: Microsoft’s AI data center push is colliding with its clean power goals
items.content: The push for new data centers at Microsoft is putting its key clean power goals at risk.

items.title: Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
items.content: The deals come as the DOD has doubled down on diversifying its exposure to AI vendors in the wake of its controversial dispute with Anthropic over usage terms of its AI models

items.title: Musk v. Altman is just getting started
items.content: Elon Musk spent the better part of three days on the witness stand this week in his lawsuit against OpenAI, and it’s already getting messy. Emails, texts, and his own tweets a

items.title: Satya Nadella says he’s ready to ‘exploit’ the new OpenAI deal
items.content: Microsoft gets to offer OpenAI's tech to its cloud customers and doesn't have to pay for it. "We fully plan to exploit it," Nadella said.

items.title: Consumers Spent More in April Despite High Gas Prices
items.content: Retail sales rose 0.5 percent despite higher prices for gas, food and other goods. But there are signs consumers are under some strain.

items.title: Trump Might Welcome Chinese Investment, but America Is Wary
items.content: A pledge for more Chinese investment could face backlash given longstanding national security concerns in the United States.

items.title: The Met Will Expand by Merging With the Nearby Neue Galerie
items.content: Beginning in 2028, the Metropolitan Museum of Art will own the Neue’s Fifth Avenue home and the prestige collection of 20th-century Austrian and German art built by Ronald S.

items.title: In Qatar, Energy Sector Damage Is Severe, and the Way Back Will Be Long
items.content: Iranian strikes and a blockade have paralyzed Qatar’s gas engine, creating a technical bottleneck likely to stall exports for years.
